ogTitle: South Golden Beach vs South Grafton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Golden Beach and South Grafton.
heading: South Golden Beach vs South Grafton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Golden Beach, New South Wales and South Grafton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Golden Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to South Grafton (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Golden Beach or South Grafton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Golden Beach has a flood risk score of 22/100, while South Grafton scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Golden Beach scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. South Grafton scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Golden Beach or South Grafton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Golden Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
